pr.title: Fix to have __start match included in configure.py
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Added the already-matched dolphin/os/init/__start.c object to the GALE01 split configuration and to configure.py's TRKLib object list, so the build/configuration system recognizes __start as a matching object. The PR body notes it had previously been matched but was not part of the file.
what_changed: Added a new split entry for dolphin/os/init/__start.c in config/GALE01/splits.txt.
Recorded __start.c as an .init-section object from start address 0x800051EC to end address 0x8000541C.
Added Object(Matching, "dolphin/os/init/__start.c") to the TRKLib object list in configure.py.
Placed __start.c before dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.c, matching the adjacent split order where __start.c ends at 0x8000541C and __ppc_eabi_init.c begins at 0x8000541C.
smart_moves: Kept the split boundaries contiguous with __ppc_eabi_init.c, avoiding a gap or overlap in the .init section.
Marked __start.c as Matching in configure.py rather than NonMatching, preserving the known matched status stated in the PR body.
Updated both the split metadata and the configure.py object registry, which prevents the common failure mode where a matched source exists but is omitted from build/decomp accounting.
decomp_lessons: When a function or object is matched, ensure it is represented in both config/GALE01/splits.txt and the relevant configure.py Library object list.
For init-section work, verify object ordering and address continuity against neighboring init objects such as __ppc_eabi_init.c.
A source file being matched is not enough for project progress tracking or build inclusion; the configuration layer must also classify it as Object(Matching, ...).
naming_conventions: Dolphin OS init startup sources use the path pattern dolphin/os/init/__name.c, including dolphin/os/init/__start.c and dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.c.
configure.py expresses matched objects with Object(Matching, "path/to/file.c") and unmatched objects with Object(NonMatching, "path/to/file.c").
The split file uses source-path headers ending in a colon, followed by indented section ranges such as .init start:0x... end:0x....
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use exact section ranges in splits.txt to bind startup/init code to the correct source file.
For adjacent low-level init objects, derive or verify boundaries from neighboring start/end addresses; here __start.c ends exactly where __ppc_eabi_init.c begins.
Classify already-matched startup code as Matching in configure.py to include it in matching progress and build configuration.
follow_up_queries: Check for other matched source files present in splits.txt but missing from configure.py object lists.
Check for other configure.py Matching objects that lack a corresponding config/GALE01/splits.txt entry.
Audit dolphin/os/init split ranges for complete .init section coverage and correct ordering.
